Klocwork Configuration CheckCompliant
ⓘ How to Read This Report

Compliance Status

Compliant  — no open issues and all waivers are active.
Conditionally Compliant  — no open or invalid issues, but one or more waivers are not yet active.
Non-Compliant  — has open issues (Analyze / Fix Planned) or invalid / missing / mismatched waivers.

Waiver Quality

A waivered issue is one whose KW status is Ignore, Defer, or Not a Problem. Waiver quality is determined by the waiver ID in the issue comment.

  • Open Issues and Invalid / Missing / Mismatched Waivers1 — contribute to Non-Compliant; rows highlighted red in the Raw Issues table.
  • Inactive Waivers2 — contribute to Conditionally Compliant; rows highlighted yellow in the Raw Issues table.
  • Active Waivers — waiver status is Deviation Active; no row highlighting (fully compliant).

1 Invalid = missing or invalid waiver ID   Mismatch = waiver checker code does not match the KW issue checker code
2 Inactive = waiver found but not yet active

CWE 2024 Top 25 GEP

CWE 2024 Top 25 Guideline Enforcement Plan — mapping of CWE IDs to applicable Klocwork checker codes with per-CWE compliance status. Compliance counts are aggregated across all applicable checker codes for each CWE ID. Rows marked No Tool Support indicate CWEs with no equivalent Klocwork checker; compliance columns are not applicable for those rows. Compliance status uses the same colour coding as the Compliance Summary table.

MCAL-37158JIRA2012-R.8.3 MISRA.OBJ.FUNC.PARAMS.IDENTDeviation ActiveMISRA CPermittedCode Quality (Portability)ProjectMISRA.OBJ.FUNC.PARAMS.IDENTThe object or function "ECAPGlobalIntEnable" has same parameter name and type in declaration and definition for the single build. Since SDK contains multiple snapshots for the same header or source files for PDK and MCAL, KlocWork reported this as violation by considering it is for same build, but actually it is not.   Providing Rule information here, once the KW issue is resolved, it can MISRA C table will be updated directly. Until then below information can be used as reference. MISRAC_Waiver Table Info: Rule : 2012-R.8.3 Checker Code : MISRA.OBJ.FUNC.PARAMS.IDENT Description : All declarations of an object or function shall use the same names and type qualifiers Category : Advisory Decidable, System

85381CanMISRA AdvisoryMISRA.ETYPE.INAPPR.CAST.2012IgnoreMCAL-10794Deviation Activec29x_mcal/drivers/Can/src/Can_Priv.c3219Permitted Deviation: MCAL-10794 These macros return values in the range of Uint32. Also, these macros use the appropriate SHIFT and bit MASK to ensure values are within the expected value range. So, casting the value returned by the macro to an enum created specifically for this register bit will not be a problem.The value of an expression of essential type 'unsigned int' is cast to an inappropriate essential type 'Essentially Enum'Can_SetOpModePriv

97496CanMISRA RequiredNUM.OVERFLOW.DFIgnoreMCAL-30310Deviation Activec29x_mcal/drivers/Can/src/Can_Priv.c3500Deviation ID: MCAL-30310 rxFIFONum cannot overflow as max value is MCAN_RX_MAX_FIFO_NUM (2), which is the hardware limit for MCAN RX FIFOs. EB Tresos configurator validates this limit at configuration generation time. Max value 2 is well within uint8 range (0-255).The expression 'msgRamConfig->rxFIFONum + 1' might result in an overflow or wraparoundCan_SetupBasicCanRxMailboxPriv

97498CanMISRA RequiredNUM.OVERFLOW.DFIgnoreMCAL-30310Deviation Activec29x_mcal/drivers/Can/src/Can_Priv.c3554Deviation ID: MCAL-30310 extFilterNum cannot overflow as max value is KMAX_EXTD_FILTERS_PER_CONTROLLER (64), which is validated by EB Tresos configurator at configuration generation time. 97499CanMISRA RequiredNUM.OVERFLOW.DFIgnoreMCAL-30310Deviation Activec29x_mcal/drivers/Can/src/Can_Priv.c3558Deviation ID: MCAL-30310 rxBuffNum cannot overflow as max value is MCAN_RX_BUFFER_MAX_NUM (64), which is the hardware limit for MCAN dedicated Rx buffers. EB Tresos configurator validates this limit at configuration generation time. Max value 64 is well within uint8 range (0-255).The expression 'msgRamConfig->rxBuffNum + 1' might result in an overflow or wraparoundCan_SetupFullCanRxMailboxPriv
97500CanMISRA RequiredNUM.OVERFLOW.DFIgnoreMCAL-30310Deviation Activec29x_mcal/drivers/Can/src/Can_Priv.c3593Deviation ID: MCAL-30310 txFIFONum cannot overflow as max value is limited by MCAN_TX_BUFFER_MAX_NUM (32), which is the hardware limit for MCAN Tx buffers. EB Tresos configurator validates this limit at configuration generation time. Max value 32 is well within uint8 range (0-255).The expression 'msgRamConfig->txFIFONum + (uint8)1U' might result in an overflow or wraparoundCan_SetupTxMailboxPriv
97501CanMISRA RequiredNUM.OVERFLOW.DFIgnoreMCAL-30310Deviation Activec29x_mcal/drivers/Can/src/Can_Priv.c3594Deviation ID: MCAL-30310 txFIFOSize cannot overflow as max value is 32, which is the hardware limit for MCAN Tx FIFO/Queue size. EB Tresos configurator validates that total Tx buffers (dedicated + FIFO) cannot exceed 32 at configuration generation time. Max value 32 is well within uint8 range (0-255).The expression 'msgRamConfig->configParams.txFIFOSize + mailboxCfg->CanHwObjectCount' might result in an overflow or wraparoundCan_SetupTxMailboxPriv
97502CanMISRA RequiredNUM.OVERFLOW.DFIgnoreMCAL-30310Deviation Activec29x_mcal/drivers/Can/src/Can_Priv.c3604Deviation ID: MCAL-30310 txBuffNum cannot overflow as max value is limited by MCAN_TX_BUFFER_MAX_NUM (32), which is the hardware limit for MCAN dedicated Tx buffers. EB Tresos configurator validates that total Tx buffers cannot exceed 32 at configuration generation time. Max value 32 is well within uint8 range (0-255).The expression 'msgRamConfig->txBuffNum + (uint8)1U' might result in an overflow or wraparoundCan_SetupTxMailboxPriv
97503CanMISRA RequiredNUM.OVERFLOW.DFIgnoreMCAL-30310Deviation Activec29x_mcal/drivers/Can/src/Can_Priv.c3605Deviation ID: MCAL-30310 configParams.txBufNum cannot overflow as max value is 32, which is the hardware limit for MCAN dedicated Tx buffers. EB Tresos configurator validates this limit at configuration generation time. Max value 32 is well within uint8 range (0-255).The expression 'msgRamConfig->configParams.txBufNum + 1' might result in an overflow or wraparoundCan_SetupTxMailboxPriv
97504CanMISRA RequiredNUM.OVERFLOW.DFIgnoreMCAL-30310Deviation Activec29x_mcal/drivers/Can/src/Can_Priv.c4031Deviation ID: MCAL-30310 getIdx cannot overflow as it is a FIFO index bounded by MCAN_RX_FIFO_0_MAX_NUM/MCAN_RX_FIFO_1_MAX_NUM (64). The loop is bounded by fillLvl which is read from hardware register and cannot exceed 64. Max value 64 is well within uint8 range (0-255).The expression 'fifoStatus->getIdx + 1' might result in an overflow or wraparoundCan_ReadRxFIFOPriv

103640SpiMISRA RequiredNUM.OVERFLOW.DFIgnoreMCAL-30310Deviation Activec29x_mcal/drivers/Spi/src/Spi_Priv.c1761Waiver ID MCAL-30310 it's a false positive since the loop is bounded by channelPerJobThe expression 'jobObj->curChIdx + 1' might result in an overflow or wraparoundSpi_ProcessChCompletion
103641SpiMISRA RequiredNUM.OVERFLOW.DFIgnoreMCAL-30310Deviation Activec29x_mcal/drivers/Spi/src/Spi_Priv.c1803Waiver ID MCAL-30310 The expression is inherently bounded by the sequence's job count which is validated during configuration. The decrement is always preceded by a job completion event, ensuring numJobsPending > 0 at the point of decrement. This is a false positive from static analysis that cannot detect the runtime invariant maintained by the SPI driver's state machine.The expression 'seqObj->numJobsPending - 1' might result in an overflow or wraparoundSpi_ProcessJobCompletion
103642SpiMISRA RequiredNUM.OVERFLOW.DFIgnoreMCAL-30310Deviation Activec29x_mcal/drivers/Spi/src/Spi_Priv.c2585Waiver ID MCAL-30310 The expression is inherently bounded by the sequence's job count which is validated during configuration. The synchronous transmit path processes jobs sequentially, ensuring numJobsPending > 0 at each decrement point. This is a false positive from static analysis that cannot detect the runtime invariant maintained by the SPI driver's synchronous transfer logic.The expression 'seqObj->numJobsPending - 1' might result in an overflow or wraparoundSpi_ScheduleAllJobsSyncTransmitPriv
103643SpiMISRA RequiredNUM.OVERFLOW.DFIgnoreMCAL-30310Deviation Activec29x_mcal/drivers/Spi/src/Spi_Priv.c2598Waiver ID MCAL-30310 The expression is inherently bounded by the sequence's job count which is validated during configuration. The synchronous transmit path processes jobs sequentially, ensuring numJobsPending > 0 at each decrement point. This is a false positive from static analysis that cannot detect the runtime invariant maintained by the SPI driver's synchronous transfer logic.The expression 'seqObj->numJobsPending - 1' might result in an overflow or wraparoundSpi_ScheduleAllJobsSyncTransmitPriv

Deviation type: Tool Issue (KW false positive or False Diagnosis of a violation) value of reaction can be either WDG_GENERATE_RESET or WDG_GENERATE_INTERRUPT and is configurableExpression 'reaction == WDG_GENERATE_INTERRUPT' used in the condition always yields the same resultWdg_CheckReactionParam
